The Team Challenge

Friendly competition. No grade pressure. Pizza at stake.

How It Works

You'll be placed on a team of 5–6 students at the start of the term. Teams stay the same all quarter. Throughout the course, your team earns points across four categories. A scoreboard is shown at the start of each week so you can track your standing.

The team challenge does not affect your course grade — it's designed to build community and add some friendly competition. The winning team at the end of the term earns a celebration.

How Your Team Earns Points

1
Pair Coding 1 point

Your team earns a point when all (or nearly all) members submit the in-class exercise. Happens each class session (~16 total).

1
Assignments 1 point

Your team earns a point when all members submit the assignment on time. 8 assignments total.

1
Quizzes 1 point

The top 2 teams by average quiz score each earn a point. Quizzes have unlimited retakes and your best score counts — but the team competition gives you extra incentive. 10 quizzes total.

2
Fun Challenges 2 points

Complete a short weekly team challenge outside of class. One person submits for the group. 10 challenges total. These are designed to be fun and take 30 minutes or less.

"Nearly all" means all but one. If one teammate is absent, it won't tank your team's pair coding or assignment points. Life happens.

Effort over performance. Three of the four categories reward showing up and doing the work, not getting the right answer.

Fun Challenges

Each week, a new challenge will be posted on Canvas. These are designed to reinforce what you're learning in a low-stakes, creative way. Formats include predicting what code will produce, finding bugs in broken code, answering questions about a dataset, redesigning ugly plots, and R trivia.

Challenges are posted each week and due the following Sunday at 11:59 PM. One team member submits on Canvas for the group.

FAQ

What if someone drops the course?

Your team won't be penalized. Pair coding and assignment points still use the "all but one" threshold, and averages adjust automatically.

What if there's a tie at the end?

If two or more teams are tied for first place, they all win. More pizza for everyone.

Can I switch teams?

Teams are set for the term. Part of the challenge is learning to work with your teammates.

Do I have to participate?

The challenge doesn't affect your grade, but everything that earns challenge points (pair coding, assignments, quizzes) is already part of your coursework. The fun challenges are the only extra thing, and they're short.
